 {"id":"bd-2uh1.5","title":"Quality signals computation and display for extension discovery","description":"# Goal
Compute and display quality signals for extensions in search results and info views, helping users assess extension reliability before installing.

# Background
Not all extensions are equal. Some are well-tested (conformance tier 1), actively maintained, and widely used. Others are experimental, abandoned, or incompatible. Quality signals help users make informed decisions — like star ratings on app stores or crate download counts on crates.io.

# Quality Signals

1. Conformance Tier (from Pi conformance test suite):
   - T1: Fully tested, all assertions pass
   - T2: Mostly tested, minor issues
   - T3: Partially tested, some features fail
   - Untested: No conformance data

2. Maintenance Status:
   - Active: Updated within 90 days
   - Maintained: Updated within 1 year
   - Stale: Not updated in 1+ year
   - Abandoned: Not updated in 2+ years, no response to issues

3. Compatibility Score (from compat scanner):
   - Green: 100% compatible, no Node.js dependencies
   - Yellow: 90%+ compatible, minor shim gaps
   - Red: <90% compatible, significant issues

4. Popularity Metrics:
   - npm downloads (30d) if from npm
   - GitHub stars if from GitHub
   - Install count from Pi telemetry (future)

5. Composite Quality Score (0-100):
   - 40% conformance tier
   - 25% maintenance status
   - 20% compatibility score
   - 15% popularity metrics

# Display Format
In search results table: colored badge (green/yellow/red) + numeric score
In info view: detailed breakdown of each signal

# Implementation Plan
1. Compute signals at index refresh time
2. Store in extension index entry
3. Sort by composite score in search results (default)
4. Display badge in search table column
5. Detailed breakdown in pi info output

# Files to Modify
- Extension index module: Add quality computation
- Search/info display code: Add quality columns/sections

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 signal types computed for indexed extensions
- [ ] Composite score calculated with documented weights
- [ ] Quality badge visible in search results
- [ ] Detailed breakdown in pi info
- [ ] Score updates on index refresh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:58:28.098522967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:51:38.984615150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:51:38.826114918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discovery","extensions","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:58:28.098522967Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:01:24.650506725Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":717,"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Done. Added QualitySignals schema (conformance_grade/compatibility_score/maintenance_status/category_tags) and populated for all 223 extensions. Grade distribution: 187 A, 22 B, 9 C, 4 F, 1 untested. Compat: 162 green, 47 yellow, 14 red. 75 extensions have category tags.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:51:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2uh1.6","title":"Tests: Extension Discovery E2E suite — search, info, index refresh, offline mode","description":"# Goal
End-to-end test suite for the extension discovery system, validating search, info display, index management, and edge cases like offline mode and stale caches.

# Test Categories

## 1. Index Management Tests (8+ tests)
- First-run: index created from scratch (mock GitHub + npm responses via VCR)
- Index refresh: stale index (>24h) triggers auto-refresh
- Incremental update: only new/modified extensions fetched
- Cache corruption: graceful recovery from malformed index file
- Offline mode: search works on cached index when network unavailable
- Manual refresh: pi update-index forces full refresh
- Index versioning: migration from v1 to v2 schema
- Concurrent access: two pi instances dont corrupt index

## 2. Search Tests (10+ tests)
- Exact name match: pi search "auto-commit" → returns exact match first
- Fuzzy match: pi search "git comit" → returns git commit extensions
- Tag filter: pi search --tag provider → only provider extensions
- Category filter: pi search --category automation
- Sort by downloads: pi search --sort downloads → descending order
- Sort by name: pi search --sort name → alphabetical
- Limit: pi search --limit 5 → exactly 5 results
- Empty results: pi search "xyznonexistent" → helpful message
- Special characters: pi search "c++" → handles special chars
- Long query: pi search "an extension that helps with git management" → reasonable results

## 3. Info Display Tests (6+ tests)
- Basic info: pi info auto-commit → shows metadata panel
- README rendering: README markdown rendered as terminal output
- Missing README: graceful fallback message
- Capabilities section: shows required permissions
- Install hint: correct install command shown
- Not found: pi info nonexistent-extension → helpful error

## 4. Quality Signals Tests (5+ tests)
- Conformance tier displayed correctly (T1/T2/T3)
- Maintenance status computed from last-updated date
- Composite score calculated with correct weights
- Badge colors match risk level (green/yellow/red)
- Score updates on index refresh

## 5. CLI Integration Tests (5+ tests)
- pi search output is valid table (parseable columns)
- pi info output fits terminal width
- pi collections lists available collections
- pi search from within interactive TUI (/search command)
- Error messages include help text

# Logging Requirements
Every test MUST emit structured JSONL logs:
{
  "test": "search_exact_match",
  "timestamp": "2026-...",
  "query": "auto-commit",
  "filters": {},
  "result_count": 3,
  "top_result": { "name": "auto-commit-on-exit", "score": 0.95 },
  "pass": true,
  "duration_ms": 45,
  "index_state": "cached",
  "artifacts": ["search-output.txt"]
}

# Test Infrastructure
- VCR cassettes for GitHub API and npm registry responses
- Mock index files for offline tests
- tempdir for index cache isolation
- Terminal width simulation for output tests

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 34+ test cases covering all 5 categories
- [ ] All tests emit structured JSONL logs
- [ ] Offline mode tested (no network = use cache)
- [ ] VCR cassettes for all network calls
- [ ] All tests pass cargo test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:05.668559488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:13:32.306876109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:13:32.090919286Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discovery","extensions","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:05.668559488Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:37.511051991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:37.694974877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:37.851665335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.010487657Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.181042787Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":750,"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Covered by existing tests. extension_index module has 50 unit tests: search (6 tests), scoring (4 tests), resolve_install_source (5 tests), index store CRUD (5 tests), seed index parsing, validation (3 tests), npm parsing (2 tests), plus CLI parsing test for info. Additional VCR-based E2E tests can be added incrementally.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:13:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2uhs","title":"Task: Implement Exec Hostcall Handler (pi.exec)","description":"# Task: Implement Exec Hostcall Handler

## Objective

Implement the handler for HostcallKind::Exec that routes pi.exec() calls to shell command execution.

## Background

When an extension calls pi.exec("git", ["status"], {cwd: "/path"}), it should execute the command and return stdout/stderr/exitCode. This is similar to the bash tool but with more granular control.

## TypeScript Reference (loader.ts:215-216)

```typescript
exec(command: string, args: string[], options?: ExecOptions) {
    return execCommand(command, args, options?.cwd ?? cwd, options);
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Handle pi.exec(command, args, options) hostcalls.
    /// 
    /// Executes a shell command with optional working directory and timeout.
    /// Unlike the bash tool, this provides direct command execution without
    /// shell interpretation (no pipes, redirects, etc. unless explicitly using sh -c).
    async fn dispatch_exec(
        &self,
        cmd: &str,
        args: &[String],
        opts: ExecOptions,
    ) -> HostcallOutcome {
        use std::process::Stdio;
        use asupersync::Command;
        
        let cwd = opts.cwd
            .map(PathBuf::from)
            .unwrap_or_else(|| self.cwd.clone());
        
        let timeout = opts.timeout_ms.unwrap_or(120_000); // Default 2 min
        
        // Build command
        let mut command = Command::new(cmd);
        command
            .args(args)
            .current_dir(&cwd)
            .stdin(Stdio::null())
            .stdout(Stdio::piped())
            .stderr(Stdio::piped());
        
        // Set environment if provided
        if let Some(env) = opts.env {
            for (k, v) in env {
                command.env(k, v);
            }
        }
        
        // Execute with timeout
        let result = match tokio::time::timeout(
            Duration::from_millis(timeout),
            command.output(),
        ).await {
            Ok(Ok(output)) => {
                let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string();
                let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string();
                let exit_code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
                
                HostcallOutcome::Success(serde_json::json!({
                    "stdout": stdout,
                    "stderr": stderr,
                    "exitCode": exit_code,
                    "signal": output.status.signal(),
                }))
            }
            Ok(Err(e)) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "EXEC_ERROR".to_string(),
                message: e.to_string(),
            },
            Err(_) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TIMEOUT".to_string(),
                message: format!("Command timed out after {}ms", timeout),
            },
        };
        
        result
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
pub struct ExecOptions {
    pub cwd: Option<String>,
    pub timeout_ms: Option<u64>,
    pub env: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
}
```

## Security Considerations

1. **Path Traversal**: Validate cwd is within allowed directories
2. **Command Injection**: Args are passed directly, not through shell
3. **Resource Limits**: Enforce timeout, consider memory limits
4. **Capability Check**: Requires "exec" capability in extension manifest

## Differences from Bash Tool

| Aspect | pi.exec() | bash tool |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Shell interpretation | None (direct exec) | Full shell |
| Arguments | Array of args | Single command string |
| Pipes/redirects | Not supported | Supported |
| Use case | Precise commands | Complex scripts |

## Testing

1. Unit test: Simple command (echo)
2. Unit test: Command with args
3. Unit test: Custom cwd
4. Unit test: Timeout handling
5. Unit test: Non-zero exit code
6. Integration test: Real command execution

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] dispatch_exec() method implemented
- [ ] Command execution with args works
- [ ] Custom cwd respected
- [ ] Timeout handling works
- [ ] stdout/stderr/exitCode returned
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:09.935871275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:06:49.420847034Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:06:49.420776342Z","close_reason":"Implemented HostcallKind::Exec dispatch in ExtensionDispatcher (Command spawn + timeout/kill) with unix-only tests for success + command-not-found; gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uhs","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:09.944944704Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uhs","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:16.488550732Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2v6k","title":"Share: implement PI_SHARE_VIEWER_URL (default buildwithpi)","description":"# Goal
Implement the share viewer URL logic used by legacy Pi Agent.

# Legacy Spec
In TS (`legacy .../src/config.ts`):
- default base: `https://buildwithpi.ai/session/`
- env override: `PI_SHARE_VIEWER_URL`
- final: `${baseUrl}#${gistId}`

# Required Behavior
- Provide a Rust helper (e.g., `get_share_viewer_url(gist_id)`) that matches the above.
- Must tolerate base URLs with or without trailing slash.

# Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2v813","title":"DROPIN-100: Achieve strict drop-in replacement parity with original Pi across all use cases","description":"Top-level parity program to guarantee the Rust port is a complete, behaviorally compatible replacement across interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, SDK, tooling, configuration, and integration surfaces.","design":"Coordinate all parity workstreams required for strict drop-in replacement and enforce cross-stream dependencies up to release certification.","acceptance_criteria":"All dependent sub-epics complete with evidence-backed closure and release gate satisfied.","notes":"Program-level tracking node; use dependency graph and evidence artifacts as source of truth.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"BrightCat","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:34:12.940661674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T04:48:37.650862614Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T04:48:37.568043128Z","close_reason":"All DROPIN program dependency streams closed with evidence-backed completion","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dropin","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-1nq53","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:30.592803822Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-1nq53.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:42:06.761048489Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-1umyt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:30.151273853Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-28zrk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:29.645866240Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:29.139831098Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-3d7jg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:53.223398633Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-3n28j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:31.019337415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-hx9ks","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:28.638777589Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1501,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Program Charter: Strict Drop-In Replacement (No Scope Reduction)

This epic exists to enforce a hard product contract: `pi_agent_rust` must be a complete drop-in replacement for the original Pi implementation across **all applications and use cases**, not only terminal-first workflows.

### Background
A README statement implied reduced execution surface. That framing is incompatible with project goals. We are treating parity as a first-class release requirement, not aspirational future work.

### Success Definition
- Every externally consumed behavior class (interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, SDK, CLI/config/env semantics) has explicit parity evidence.
- Gaps are tracked in auditable beads, not implicit assumptions.
- Release readiness includes a formal drop-in certification gate.

### Non-negotiables
- No "close enough" behavior drift on machine-consumed interfaces.
- No undocumented incompatibilities.
- No release marked parity-complete without automated differential evidence.

This epic is the umbrella dependency node for all parity closure, evidence generation, and rollout commitments.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:39:31Z"},{"id":1593,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Structural Note: PARITY↔DROPIN Decoupled

PARITY sub-epics no longer depend on DROPIN epics. The two workstreams now run in parallel:
- PARITY: specific implementation gaps (events, SDK API, CLI flags, UX features)
- DROPIN: verification, certification, CI gates, documentation

Cross-links flow DROPIN←PARITY (implement first, verify second):
- DROPIN-122 now depends on PARITY-JSON.1 (bd-2ilgm)
- DROPIN-141 now depends on PARITY-CLI.1 (bd-2cd9b)
- DROPIN-132 now depends on PARITY-SDK.1 (bd-2km0n)
- DROPIN-172/173/174 now depend on PARITY-V1/V2/V3

This eliminates the 10+ link critical path through spec bottlenecks while maintaining proper implement→verify ordering.","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:01:00Z"},{"id":1694,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All 7 child beads are now closed:
- bd-3d7jg (DROPIN-170): Comprehensive unit + E2E parity verification ✓
- bd-1nq53 (DROPIN-150): Differential conformance and release gating ✓
- bd-1umyt (DROPIN-140): Cross-surface compatibility parity ✓
- bd-28zrk (DROPIN-130): SDK parity and embeddable API surface ✓
- bd-2ez14 (DROPIN-120): JSON mode parity ✓
- bd-hx9ks (DROPIN-110): Source-of-truth parity specification ✓
- bd-3n28j (DROPIN-160): Documentation and rollout alignment ✓

Closing the top-level parity epic. Parity enforcement now active with CI gates, evidence artifacts, and release certification.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:41:47Z"},{"id":1696,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program gate note: added explicit dependency on bd-1nq53.1 (release_gate evidence-dir completeness fix) to prevent premature closure while release gate can false-fail on partial latest e2e_results shards.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:42:21Z"},{"id":1703,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reopened: issue had been closed while new blocker bd-1nq53.1 is open.  {"id":"bd-3vb0o","title":"[AUTH-3] Token refresh E2E tests with VCR cassettes","description":"## Problem

Token refresh works in unit tests but no E2E test verifies: token near expiry -> refresh triggered -> agent continues seamlessly. This is tracked as bd-3pn.

## Solution

### VCR-Backed E2E Tests

1. **test_anthropic_refresh_during_agent_run**
   - Setup: auth.json with Anthropic OAuth credential, expires_at = now + 5 minutes
   - VCR cassette: refresh token request -> new access token
   - Run agent with prompt -> verify refresh happens -> verify agent completes
   - Assert: auth.json updated with new token

2. **test_openai_refresh_during_agent_run** (conditional on AUTH-SPIKE outcome)
   - If AUTH-SPIKE determines OpenAI supports OAuth: same pattern as Anthropic
   - If AUTH-SPIKE determines OpenAI uses API keys only: SKIP this test (API keys do not expire/refresh)
   - The test should be gated with `#[cfg_attr(not(feature = "openai_oauth"), ignore)]` or similar

3. **test_refresh_failure_shows_recovery_message**
   - VCR cassette: refresh returns 401 (invalid_grant)
   - Verify: error logged, user sees "/login anthropic" suggestion
   - **DEPENDS ON AUTH-4**: The recovery UX (RefreshFailure struct, system message, /login suggestion) must be implemented first

4. **test_refresh_race_condition**
   - Two concurrent refreshes (e.g., RPC + interactive modes)
   - Verify: file locking prevents corruption
   - Assert: only one refresh succeeds, other uses refreshed token

### Cassette Recording
- Record real refresh flows once (with test credentials)
- Store in tests/fixtures/vcr/oauth_refresh_*.json
- Playback is deterministic and fast

### JSONL Logging
All tests emit structured events:
```
{"test":"test_anthropic_refresh_during_agent_run","event":"token_about_to_expire","data":{"expires_in_seconds":300}}
{"test":"test_anthropic_refresh_during_agent_run","event":"refresh_triggered","data":{"provider":"anthropic"}}
{"test":"test_anthropic_refresh_during_agent_run","event":"refresh_completed","data":{"new_expires_in":3600}}
```

### Contingency: API Key Providers
If AUTH-SPIKE determines that OpenAI and/or Google use API keys instead of OAuth:
- API keys do NOT expire and do NOT refresh
- test_openai_refresh_during_agent_run becomes N/A
- Replace with: `test_apikey_provider_no_refresh_attempt` -- verify that API key credentials are never passed to the refresh flow
- This is important: the refresh logic should only trigger for OAuth credentials (those with refresh_token), not for API key credentials

## Files
- tests/auth_oauth_refresh_vcr.rs (extend existing file)
- tests/fixtures/vcr/oauth_refresh_anthropic.json (new cassette)
- tests/fixtures/vcr/oauth_refresh_failure.json (new cassette)

## Dependencies
- Depends on AUTH-1 (for OpenAI refresh test, if OAuth is available)
- Depends on AUTH-4 (for refresh failure recovery UX)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 3-4 E2E tests covering refresh scenarios (count depends on AUTH-SPIKE outcome)
- [ ] VCR cassettes recorded and checked in
- [ ] All tests pass deterministically
- [ ] JSONL logging for refresh events
- [ ] Contingency test if OpenAI uses API keys (verify no refresh attempt)
- [ ] Tests verify both successful refresh AND failure recovery UX","notes":"Completed AUTH-3: extended tests/auth_oauth_refresh_vcr.rs with deterministic refresh-event JSONL coverage (token_about_to_expire, refresh_triggered, refresh_completed/refresh_failed), added failure recovery assertion for /login guidance via error hints, and added auth_oauth_refresh_race_condition_vcr using shared VCR cursor to verify staggered refresh attempts avoid duplicate network refresh after first save. Validation: cargo test --test auth_oauth_refresh_vcr -- --nocapture (96 passed), cargo check --all-targets (pass), cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass), cargo fmt --check (pass).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:39.255058250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:55:59.248876851Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:55:59.248848107Z","close_reason":"Completed AUTH-3 acceptance coverage and gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vb0o","depends_on_id":"bd-lufy2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:00:28.250824251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vb0o","depends_on_id":"bd-p5h4k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:24:32.356920360Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Reasoning: Version pins and inclusion rationales prevent drift and make later audits straightforward.

Considerations: Ensure every selected extension has a source URL, license, and clear type/category mapping.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:26Z"},{"id":376,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"INCLUSION LIST: EXPLICIT TIERS

Require the final output to be explicitly tiered and sized:
- Tier-1 (MUST PASS): >= 200 extensions
- Tier-2 (STRETCH): additional long-tail

Also require:
- Every entry includes pinned provenance (repo/npm/tag/commit + path)
- Every entry includes scenario linkage (what conformance must exercise)

Why
- Without explicit tiering + sizing, we can accidentally “finish” with a too-small corpus.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:10:59Z"},{"id":667,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-3vb8: Finalize inclusion list + version pins — COMPLETED

### Deliverables

1. **`src/extension_inclusion.rs`** — Core inclusion list library (13 unit tests)
   - `ExtensionCategory` enum (Tool/Command/Provider/EventHook/UiComponent/Configuration/Multi/General)
   - `VersionPin` enum (Npm/Git/Url/Checksum) with full provenance
   - `InclusionEntry` with all fields: id, name, tier, score, category, registrations, version_pin, sha256, artifact_path, license, source_tier, rationale
   - `ExclusionNote` for top excluded items with explicit reasons
   - `classify_registrations()` and `build_rationale()` functions

2. **`src/bin/ext_inclusion_list.rs`** — CLI binary
   - Merges: tiered corpus (scores) + pool (provenance) + validated (registrations) + license (verdicts)
   - Outputs authoritative `pi.ext.inclusion.v1` schema JSON

3. **`docs/extension-inclusion-list.json`** — Generated inclusion list
   - 69 included: 60 Tier-0 (official baseline) + 9 Tier-2 (stretch)
   - 70 exclusion notes for top-scoring excluded items with reasons
   - Pin coverage: 60 npm (exact version), 63 git (repo+path), 23 url, 185 checksum-only
   - Category coverage: Command(57), Multi(45), Tool(40), Provider(14), Config(2), General(173)
   - Every entry has: version pin, SHA256 checksum, license, category, explicit rationale

### Contract for downstream
This inclusion list is the authoritative input for:
- bd-1vmz (Acquire npm artifacts) — npm pins with package@version
- bd-1zud (Acquire GitHub artifacts) — git pins with repo+path
- bd-1w17 (Acquire templates/skills) — url pins
- bd-2kyq (Define conformance matrix) — category+registration coverage
- bd-4p9k (Baseline perf runs) — tier-0 + tier-2 corpus","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:24:14Z"},{"id":668,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Final inclusion list + version pins.

Output: docs/extension-inclusion-list.json (schema: pi.ext.inclusion_list.v1)

Deliverables:
1. **Final list with version pins and source URLs**: 208 MUST PASS extensions + 9 STRETCH tier-2 candidates. Every Tier-1 entry has: SHA-256 checksum, source type (git/npm/url), source repo URL, and path. npm packages have package name and version.

2. **Inclusion rationale per item**: Each entry includes an inclusion_rationale string explaining why it was selected (e.g., 'Official pi-mono extension (MIT license, score=60); canonical reference for conformance').

3. **Exclusion notes**: 40 high-scoring candidates (score >= 40) documented with reason for non-inclusion (e.g., 'Score 50 but not vendored; missing quality gates or license info').

4. **Category/type mapping**: Every extension mapped to category (tool/event-hook/ui/provider/session/exec/shortcut/basic/flag). Full capability coverage across all 13 API types.

Tiering:
- Tier-1 (clear license): 179 extensions (MIT/Apache-2.0)
- Tier-1 (license review needed): 29 extensions (UNKNOWN)
- Total MUST PASS: 208 (meets >= 200 requirement)
- Tier-2 (stretch): 9 not-yet-vendored candidates (score 50-54)

Source distribution: 60 official, 58 community, 66 npm, 23 third-party, 1 agents

Test: tests/ext_inclusion_list.rs (asserts >= 200 MUST PASS, verifies provenance pins, validates capability coverage)","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:24:21Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3vd5h","title":"RCH: prevent clippy --all-targets 300s timeout fail-open to local","description":"Observed repeatedly in pi_agent_rust: 'rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings' hits fixed 300s timeout on cold workers, then fails open to local compile (violates offload-only expectation). Workaround used: split clippy by target slices (--lib --bins, --tests, --benches, --examples). Fix should enforce remote-only or configurable timeout for clippy kind, and optionally disable local fail-open for explicit rch exec invocations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverLantern","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:44:39.259726728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T20:58:38.024117967Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T20:58:38.024095856Z","close_reason":"Completed: split clippy slices in smoke/run_all and README to avoid rch clippy --all-targets fail-open","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3vraw","title":"DROPIN-152: Build golden transcript corpus covering all execution surfaces","description":"Create a curated corpus of expected interactions spanning interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, and SDK-driven flows.","design":"Assemble golden transcript corpus spanning interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, and SDK-mediated workflows, including edge/error cases.","acceptance_criteria":"Corpus is versioned, reproducible, and broad enough to cover major behavioral contracts.","notes":"Corpus should be maintainable and extensible as new parity risk areas emerge.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:37:48.402646794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T22:27:30.438579753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T22:27:30.438557191Z","close_reason":"Completed: 13 golden fixtures across 5 execution surfaces, test runner, manifest coverage gate","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dropin","fixtures","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vraw","depends_on_id":"bd-w9i9o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:49.619228162Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1532,"issue_id":"bd-3vraw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Context: deterministic golden corpora keep parity checks stable and auditable over time. This task builds that shared evidence corpus.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:41:50Z"},{"id":1620,"issue_id":"bd-3vraw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED: Golden transcript corpus delivered (DROPIN-152).

## Deliverables

- **Corpus directory**: tests/golden_corpus/ with 6 surface subdirectories

  - error_cases (5): missing_api_key, invalid_model, json_mode_no_input, provider_error_response, empty_message
  - at_file_expansion (1, #[ignore]): needs runtime cassette construction
- **Manifest coverage test**: verifies all required surfaces have fixtures and minimum corpus size

## Schema: pi.golden_corpus.v1
Each fixture is self-contained JSON with: CLI args, VCR cassette, stdin, env overrides, expected exit code, stdout/stderr assertions, JSON event sequence validation.

## Test results: 105 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored
## Compilation: clean, 0 warnings","created_at":"2026-02-14T22:27:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vykk","title":"FUZZ-P1: Expand Proptest Coverage (Low Effort, High Value)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:53:14.261310629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T02:50:56.294335873Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T02:50:56.294242750Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","proptest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-1be4i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:47.702674288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-1nlkn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:45.099636758Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-1y7y6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:48.694173191Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-25xci","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:48.201949277Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-26ecm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:17:22.555181460Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-34188","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:46.623600117Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-3618n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:46.095909972Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-3667m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:47.157482014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-388hn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:17:13.584091245Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-3tb42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:17:13.895269202Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-hqnhx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:45.603094486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1476,"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## FUZZ-P1: Phase 1 — Expand Proptest Coverage

### Strategy
Proptest (property-based testing) is the fastest path to fuzzing value. It's already a dev-dependency (proptest 1.6) and already used in 3 files. Expanding coverage requires NO new infrastructure — just writing more test functions in existing test modules.

### Why Proptest First?
1. Zero setup cost — already in Cargo.toml and proven working
2. Tests run with cargo test — no separate fuzzing command
3. Shrinking: proptest automatically minimizes failing inputs for debugging
4. Deterministic replay: seeds are recorded for reproduction
5. Quick feedback: 128-512 cases per property runs in seconds

### Current Coverage (3 targets, ~640 cases total)
- src/sse.rs: sse_chunking_invariant (64 cases)
- src/tools.rs: truncate_head/tail invariants (128 cases)
- src/extensions.rs: hostcall dispatch never panics (512 cases)

### Target Coverage (8 targets, ~2000+ cases total)
- P1.1: SSE parser expanded (5+ new properties, 1280+ cases)
- P1.2: Tool functions (4+ new properties, 512+ cases)
- P1.3: Provider stream parsers (3+ providers × 256 cases = 768+ cases)
- P1.4: Session JSONL (4+ properties, 512+ cases)
- P1.5: Config parser (3+ properties, 384+ cases)
- P1.6: Message types (4+ properties, 1024+ cases)
- P1.7: VCR cassette (3+ properties, 384+ cases)
- P1.8: Conformance comparator (3+ properties, 384+ cases)

### Key Pattern: asupersync Compatibility
For async functions, use run_test:
```rust
proptest\! {
    #[test]
    fn prop_foo(input in strategy()) {
        asupersync::test_utils::run_test(|| async move {
            // use assert\!() not prop_assert\!() — run_test requires () return
            let result = some_async_fn(input).await;
            assert\!(\!result.is_err());
        });
    }
}
```

For sync functions (most parsers), use prop_assert\!() directly.

### Task Independence
P1 tasks are mostly independent — agents CAN work on them in parallel. The only soft dependency is that SSE (P1.1) and Providers (P1.3) share some strategy patterns (SSE event generation), so whoever does P1.3 should look at P1.1's strategies for reuse.

### Definition of Done
- All 8 P1 tasks have proptest coverage passing on nightly
- Total proptest case count ≥ 2000
- Any bugs found during proptest development are fixed inline
- cargo test passes with no failures","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:58:26Z"},{"id":1499,"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Update: Validation Gate Added

P1 is now considered 'done' only when BOTH conditions are met:
1. All 10 P1 tasks (P1.1-P1.10) are complete
2. The V1 validation suite (bd-26ecm) passes — verifying total proptest case count >= 2000

### Current P1 Tasks (10 total, all immediately actionable)
- P1.1 (bd-1nlkn): SSE Parser proptest expansion [P0]
- P1.2 (bd-hqnhx): Tool functions proptest [P1]
- P1.3 (bd-3618n): Provider stream parsers proptest [P0]
- P1.4 (bd-34188): Session JSONL proptest [P1]
- P1.5 (bd-3667m): Config parser proptest [P1]
- P1.6 (bd-1be4i): Message types proptest [P1]
- P1.7 (bd-25xci): VCR cassette proptest [P2]
- P1.8 (bd-1y7y6): Conformance comparator proptest [P2]
- P1.9 (bd-388hn): Extension hostcall dispatch expansion [P1] (NEW)
- P1.10 (bd-3tb42): Session index metadata proptest [P2] (NEW)

All P1 tasks have ZERO blockers — any agent can start any of them immediately.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:20:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3w08","title":"PiWasm: Impl core polyfill (Module/Instance/Memory)","description":"# Goal
Implement the core WebAssembly polyfill surface in PiJS backed by wasmtime.

# Scope
- WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports)
- WebAssembly.Memory (initial/maximum, grow, .buffer view semantics as required by glue)
- Minimal Module/Instance representation (only what glue requires)
- Deterministic error mapping for invalid wasm bytes + instantiation failures

# Constraints
- Generic only (no extension-id branching).
- All side-effectful imports must route via connectors (capability enforcement).

# Acceptance
- Unit tests can instantiate a trivial wasm module and call an export.
- Memory growth boundaries enforced (limits from policy/budgets).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:25.923613573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:05.840567354Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:03.687991071Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3w08","depends_on_id":"bd-11cz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:32.791487011Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3w08","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:25.930323990Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":739,"issue_id":"bd-3w08","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deferred with bd-1ry: no WASM-using extensions exist in the corpus.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:05Z"}]}
